target.name: Databricks
target.founder: Ali Ghodsi, Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia, Patrick Wendell, Reynold Xin, Andy Konwinski, Arsalan Tavakoli-Shiraji

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Databricks, a Series L AI & Software company, is currently valued at $134.0B following $24.7B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 companies in the same sector and similar valuation tier, including both public and private benchmarks. Public comparables are limited to Palantir (PLTR); private peers include OpenAI ($300.0B), xAI ($250.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), and Waymo ($126.0B), among others.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the 8-company peer set stands at $154.5B, with a range spanning $29.3B to $400.0B. This suggests modest upside to Databricks' current mark. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in both business model and investor composition within the AI & Software frontier-tech universe—Palantir trades publicly at $400.0B while earlier-stage and later-stage private companies cluster between $126.0B and $300.0B.

The triangulated range derived from available methods is $131.3B (low) to $177.7B (high), with a median of $154.5B. Databricks' current $134.0B valuation falls within this band but below the peer median, implying either a modest valuation discount or uncertainty regarding comparable company representativeness. Key uncertainties include limited public trading data, heterogeneous stage alignment, and the rapid repricing dynamics characteristic of frontier-tech venture markets.
